sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: GENERAL DYNAMICS CORP는 2026-04-05 분기에 매출 134억 8,100만 달러를 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 미국 정부(DoW 및 정보기관)를 핵심 고객으로 하는 방산 및 항공우주 사업 포트폴리오를 통해 안정적인 수익 구조를 유지하고 있습니다.
항공우주 부문의 매출은 신규 항공기 인도 물량과 서비스 유형에 따라 변동하며, 생산 로트별 평균 단위 원가 산정이 운영 비용에 직접적인 영향을 미칩니다.
2026년 1분기 영업 현금흐름은 21억 5,500만 달러로, 전년 동기 148달러(사용) 대비 괄목할 만한 개선을 보였습니다.

riskChanges.text: 항공기 인도 시점과 생산 로트별 평균 원가 추정치의 변화가 분기별 운영 비용 및 수익성에 불확실성을 초래할 수 있습니다.
회계 추정 및 가정의 복잡성으로 인해 실제 운영 결과가 재무제표에 보고된 추정치와 다를 수 있는 위험이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: Combat Systems 부문의 운영 운전자본 감소가 2026년 1분기 현금흐름 개선의 주요 동인으로 작용했습니다.
전년 동기에는 항공우주 및 Combat Systems 부문의 운전자본 증가로 인해 현금흐름에 부정적인 영향이 발생한 바 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 당기 매출은 134억 8,100만 달러, 영업이익은 14억 2,000만 달러, 순이익은 11억 2,500만 달러를 기록했습니다.
총자산은 590억 2,900만 달러이며, 현금및현금성자산은 36억 5,400만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 운전자본 관리 효율성이 현금흐름의 핵심 변수로 부상한 만큼, 향후 Combat Systems 부문의 재고 및 매출채권 관리 지속 여부를 모니터링해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2026-04-05 revenue = 134억 8,100만 달러 (USD 13.481B; 13,481 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-04-05.
Quarter ended 2026-04-05 operating income = 14억 2,000만 달러 (USD 1.42B; 1,420 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-04-05.
Quarter ended 2026-04-05 net income = 11억 2,500만 달러 (USD 1.125B; 1,125 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-04-05.
Quarter ended 2026-04-05 total assets = 590억 2,900만 달러 (USD 59.029B; 59,029 million), XBRL range instant..2026-04-05.
Quarter ended 2026-04-05 cash and cash equivalents = 36억 5,400만 달러 (USD 3.654B; 3,654 million), XBRL range instant..2026-04-05.

MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS (Dollars in millions, except per-share amounts or unless otherwise noted) BUSINESS OVERVIEW General Dynamics is a global aerospace and defense company that offers a broad portfolio of products and services in business aviation; ship construction and repair; land combat vehicles, weapon systems and munitions; and technology products and services. Our company is organized into four operating segments: Aerospace, Marine Systems, Combat Systems and Technologies. We refer to the latter three collectively as our defense segments. Our primary customer is the U.S. government, including the Department of War (DoW), the intelligence community and other U.S. government agencies. We also have significant business with non-U.S. governments and a diverse base of corporate and individual buyers of business jet aircraf

Revenue associated with the segment’s services businesses is recognized as work progresses or upon delivery of services. Fluctuations in revenue from period to period result from the number and mix of new aircraft deliveries, and the level and type of aircraft services performed during the period. The majority of the Aerospace segment’s operating costs relates to new aircraft production on firm orders and consists of labor, material, subcontractor and overhead costs. The costs are accumulated in production lots, recorded in inventory and recognized as operating costs at aircraft delivery based on the estimated average unit cost in a production lot. While changes in the estimated average unit cost for a production lot impact the level of operating costs, the amount of operating costs reported in a given period is based largely on the number and type of aircraft delivered. Operating costs

Cash Flows in Part I, Item 1: 33 Three Months Ended April 5, 2026 March 30, 2025 Net cash provided/(used) by operating activities $ 2,155 $ (148) Net cash used by investing activities (202) (130) Net cash used by financing activities (629) (175) OPERATING ACTIVITIES Cash provided by operating activities was $2.2 billion in the first three months of 2026 compared with cash used by operating activities of $148 in the same period in 2025. The primary driver of cash flows in both periods was net earnings. Cash flows in the first quarter of 2026 were affected positively by reductions in operating working capital led by the Combat Systems segment. Cash flows in the first quarter of 2025 were affected negatively by growth in operating working capital, particularly driven by timing in our Aerospace and Combat Systems segments. INVESTING ACTIVITIES Cash   Normalized numeric aliases: $2.2 billion = 22억 달러; $2,155 = 2,155달러; $2.2 = 2.2달러; $148 = 148달러; $202 = 202달러; 2,155 million = 21억 5,500만 달러.

Results of Operations is based on the unaudited Consolidated Financial Statements, which have been prepared in accordance with GAAP. The preparation of financial statements in accordance with GAAP requires that we make estimates and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities and the disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the financial statements, as well as the reported amounts of revenue and expenses during the reporting period. We employ judgment in making our estimates, but they are 35 based on historical experience, currently available information and various other assumptions that we believe to be reasonable under the circumstances. Actual results may differ from these estimates. We believe our judgment is applied consistently and produces financial information that fairly depicts our results of operations for all periods presented
